why_winner: The 'Six screens in. UI looked clean. / Nothing worked.' opener is the sharpest hook of the three — concrete, creates an immediate curiosity gap in under 10 words, and earns every tweet that follows. Authenticity stays high across all four tweets; the story arc is complete and the CTA question lands naturally out of the lived experience. Variant B is also the most tweet-1-stands-alone: someone who never expands the thread still gets the full emotional hit.

runner_up_notes.C: The 'Still think one prompt gets you a working app?' closer is sharper than Variant B's question. Consider swapping B's final CTA for C's framing on a future iteration. Variant A's theorem-first opener ('AI is fast at producing output. It is slow at producing usefulness.') is the most screenshot-able single line — could serve as a standalone single-tweet post on a separate day.
